title: Weaver
subtitle: Craft a character from your idea

panel.blurb: Author characters and worlds in the studio. Finished cards land in your library.

home.title: Studio
home.empty: Nothing here yet. Hit New to start your first build.

character.title: Character
character.desc: A single person you chat with, grown from your idea.

world.title: World
world.desc: A place you roleplay in. It sets scenes and voices its people.

import.title: Import
import.desc: Bring in an existing card or worldbook and build from it.

persona.createHint: Adds a persona step before finalize. The Weaver writes a persona to play opposite this build, attached to the launched chat.

step.heading: Create a persona
step.help: A persona to play opposite this build. Generate one from the idea below, edit it, then continue to finalize.
step.ideaHelp: Optional — a name, a vibe, a few details. Leave it blank to let the Weaver invent one that fits.
step.ideaPlaceholder: e.g. Mara, a wry field medic who jokes to deflect and is fiercely loyal once you earn it…

pairing.toggle: Write an opening opposite this persona
pairing.help: An alternate greeting on the character card that opens the scene with this persona in play. Pick the relationship register so it reads right.
pairing.placeholder: The opening greeting, written opposite this persona…

review.heading: Review your persona
review.help: Edit anything before saving. The body is always in play; depth surfaces only when relevant.
review.name: Name
review.bodyHelp: One detail per line. Kept lean: this rides every turn.
review.depthHelp: Background, history, secrets — stored in a worldbook and triggered by keywords, so it never bloats every turn.

done.body: Your persona is in your library. Pick it when you start a chat, or edit it any time in Personas.

import.noteRebuild: Imports rebuild to studio quality through the normal loom.
import.noteOriginal: The original lands in your library untouched, portrait intact.
import.cardFoot: Either way, the original card lands in your library untouched.

rebuild.title: Rebuild as a Character
rebuild.desc: Reverse it into the loom, interview only its gaps, and reweave to studio quality.

world_treatment.title: Build as a World
world_treatment.desc: Its places and people become a narrator card with a lore book behind it.

enrich.title: Enrich the entries
enrich.desc: Deepen thin entries into rich ones. An entry that does not come out better keeps its original.

generate_character.title: Generate a character
generate_character.desc: Mine the book for a person and build them at full depth. The card carries the book.

build_world.title: Build a World
build_world.desc: The book becomes the lore base. The interview asks only what it cannot supply.

store.title: Store as-is
store.desc: Bring it in unchanged, ready to attach to anything.

enrichRun.label: Enriching entries
enrichRun.stopNote: Stopping keeps everything finished so far.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.empty: No sessions yet. Start one to begin.
sessions.deleteConfirm: Delete this session? This cannot be undone.

dream.heading: Your dream
dream.help: Describe your character or scenario in your own words. Rough is fine; the Weaver builds from what you write here.
dream.placeholder: Paste or write your idea here…

readback.name: Read-back
readback.desc: I lock in what's already in your dream.

interview.name: Interview
interview.desc: I ask only what you alone can decide.

bible.name: Bible
bible.desc: I weave everything into one Bible and gate it.

render.name: Render
render.desc: I write every card field, each checked alone.

readback.heading: Here's what I understood
readback.intro: I read your dream and locked in what was there. Just check I got it right, and click anything wrong to fix it.
readback.none: No read-back yet. Go back and run it from the Dream stage.
readback.willAskGroup: I'll ask you about these in the interview
readback.willMixedGroup: Part yours, part mine — I'll ask the core and write the rest
readback.willWriteGroup: I'll write these for you, from everything above
readback.pinYourself: Click if you'd rather specify this yourself
readback.rerunHint: Run the read-back again from scratch (replaces edits).

interview.heading: A few questions
interview.intro: I read your dream and ask about what's still open, one question at a time, in plain words. Answer in your own words. If you're stuck, ask for directions.
interview.introNoGaps: Your dream already pins down the essentials. I can still dig deeper, or skip ahead whenever you like.
interview.activeHelp: Answer in your own words. Each question says what it's for, and you can finish whenever you want.
interview.railNoteOpen: I'm asking about what's still open, one thing at a time.
interview.railNoteDeepening: Essentials covered. Now I'm digging deeper. Finish whenever.
interview.sparkHint: Three directions this could go. Pick one to drop it into the box, then make it yours.
interview.enhanceHint: Your words, taken further. Pick one to replace your draft.
interview.allAnswered: That's everything I needed to ask. Finish when you're ready.
interview.generateFailedBody: The model didn't return a question. Your answers so far are saved, so this picks up exactly where you left off.
interview.generateFailedNote: Nothing was lost and nothing retries on its own. Try again, or finish with what you have.
interview.doneCount: Interview complete. {{count}} answered, I'll take it from here.
interview.rerunHint: Undo your interview answers and ask again from the start.

world_agency.question: Does this world push back?
world_agency.why: Worlds can hold an agenda of their own. Most don't need one.
world_agency.offDesc: It hosts your scenes and follows your lead.
world_agency.onDesc: It pursues an agenda and holds hard lines, enforced on the card.
world_agency.note: You can change this any time on the hub.

bible.heading: The character Bible
bible.help: Everything pulled into one coherent character: your ideas, the parts I wrote to fit them, and how they connect. Checked against a quality bar before any field is written.
bible.intro: Now I'll pull everything into one coherent character: your ideas, the parts I write to fit them, and how it all connects. I check it against a quality bar before any of the character is written.
bible.gateScored: {{fail}} of {{total}} checks need work before this renders cleanly.
bible.gateAllPass: All checks pass. Ready to render.
bible.gateEditedHint: You changed the source. Re-check to score it again before rendering.
bible.flagFix: Weak on {{criteria}}. Add those specifics to the entries below, then Re-check or Rebuild.
bible.flagFixGeneric: Add the specifics called out above to the entries below, then Re-check or Rebuild.
bible.rebuildHint: Throw this away and synthesize a fresh Bible from your facts.
bible.recheckHint: Run the quality gate again on your edits.
bible.redoHint: Let the Weaver write this one entry again — the rest stays put.
bible.redoDirtyHint: Save or discard your edits first, then redo this entry.
bible.redoPlaceholder: Optional: steer this entry, e.g. darker, more specific
bible.depthHint: Deepening answers carried beyond the card. They back the character when they matter instead of bloating the card.
bible.continueFlaggedHint: This Bible is flagged. You can still render, but the fields may inherit its weaknesses.

render.heading: Write the character
render.help: Each field is written from the frozen Bible and checked on its own. A field either passes or comes back flagged with a reason. Re-render any field on its own.
render.flaggedNoContent: This field couldn't be written. Re-render it, or strengthen the Bible.
render.directFieldNote: Carried from the Bible · not model-generated
render.staleHint: Written before the Bible last changed. Re-render to refresh it.
render.nudgePlaceholder: Steer this field, e.g. leaner, more guarded
render.nudgePlaceholderList: Steer all openings, e.g. higher stakes, more distinct
render.staleBody: This field was written before the latest Bible edit. Re-render to bring it in line.
render.staleBodyEdited: This field was written before the latest Bible edit. Re-rendering will replace your hand-edit.
render.replaceEditBody: This field is hand-edited. Re-rendering overwrites your text with a fresh pass from the Bible.
render.fieldFlaggedFix: Re-render it on its own, or strengthen the Bible entries it draws from, then render again.
render.bibleFlaggedCompact: Rendered anyway so you can see the weak spots. The source never passed the bar, so expect generic results until you tighten the Bible.
render.noRetryNote: The Weaver surfaces problems and waits; it never silently retries. A weak source only makes weak output, and you hold the gate.
render.finalizeBlocked: Render and accept every field first

flag.compactNote: Surfaced, not auto-retried. You hold the gate.

finalize.heading: Finalize the card
finalize.help: Assemble the accepted fields into a real character card. Governance rides on the card, so it works under any preset.
finalize.doneHelp: The card is in your library. Governance rides on the card, so it works under any preset.
finalize.governanceBody: The card carries identity plus its own governance entries; the system prompt stays empty and no preset is needed. A constant re-anchor keeps the character on its spine across a long chat.
finalize.doneBody: Finalized from the gated Bible. The Bible stays in this session; only derivable structured data rides along for later enhancing.

bookToggle.depth: Bind a depth book to the card
bookToggle.lore: Bind the lore book to the card

bookBody.depth: Your deepening answers become a triggered worldbook bound to the card. They surface in chat when relevant and never sit on the card itself.
bookBody.lore: Your deepening answers become the world's lore book, bound to the card. Entries surface in chat when relevant, and the narrator treats them as canon.

bookError.depth: The card was created, but the depth book failed: {{error}}
bookError.lore: The card was created, but the lore book failed: {{error}}

card.title: Card
card.emptyField: Empty. Edit fields to add one.

overview.noEssence: No essence line yet — edit in Build.

portrait.sub: The character's main face. Set one as the card avatar.
portrait.promptPlaceholder: Suggest tags from the card, or write your own (e.g. 1girl, long_hair, ...)
portrait.suggestFailed: Could not suggest tags. Check the text connection and try again.
portrait.noConnections: Add an image connection in settings first.
portrait.emptyCanvas: No portrait yet. Suggest tags and generate.
portrait.genFailed: Generation failed. Check the image connection and try again.
portrait.commitFailed: Could not set the avatar. Try again.

visual.portraitSub: The character's main face — becomes the card avatar.
visual.expressionsSub: A named emotion set, kept consistent from the portrait seed.
visual.scenesSub: Free-form scene & background images, seeded from the scenario.
visual.alternatesSub: Alternate portraits you keep around — promote any to the live avatar.
visual.comingSoon: Image generation lands in the next step — the studio shell is ready.

expressions.sub: Derived from the committed portrait so the face and style hold. Commit a cell and chat picks it up.
expressions.noPortrait: Commit a portrait first. Expressions are derived from it.
expressions.gateGeneric: This connection cannot take an image as input, so it cannot hold the portrait's face.

mechanism.edit: This connection edits the portrait directly.
mechanism.reference: This connection generates with the portrait as a character reference.
mechanism.init: This connection runs the portrait through img2img. Strength is under Advanced.

expressions.editNoPrompt: No tag prompt needed: the portrait carries the character and the instruction carries the expression.
expressions.basePromptPlaceholder: Tags that pin the character's look. The expression tags are added per cell.
expressions.commitFailed: The expression could not be committed

hub.noEntries: No entries yet. Answers you add land here.
hub.noBook: No lore book yet. Add lore and one is created for this world.
hub.noQuestion: No question right now. Try again.
hub.questionFailed: Could not get a question. Check the connection and try again.
hub.answerFailed: The answer could not be saved. Try again.
hub.loadFailed: Could not load this world's hub.

people.empty: No people yet. Anyone you name while building a world lands here on its own. Propose asks the world to suggest people from its lore, or add someone yourself.
people.extraTitle: Write a couple of background lines, so the narrator can use them in passing
people.weaveTitle: Answer a few short questions about them, then save a profile the narrator speaks them from
people.reweaveTitle: Answer more questions and rewrite their profile
people.weaveFootEmpty: Answer at least one question, then save. The result is a profile the narrator speaks them from.
people.weaveHintMore: More answers make a sharper profile. Save any time.
people.weaveHintReady: Enough to save a solid profile. You can always answer more later.
people.proposeFailed: The world could not propose people. Try again.
people.fleshFailed: Could not flesh the extra. Try again.
people.weaveFailed: The weave failed. Your answers are safe. Try again.
people.promoteTitle: Build them into their own character card, tied to this world
people.promoteFailed: Promotion could not start. Try again.
people.inUniverseHint: their own cards, tied to this world
people.inUniverseEmpty: No one yet. When someone in the roster grows beyond a side character, promote them. They become their own character card that knows this world, and it lands here.
people.rosterHint: the people the narrator can bring into a scene
people.interviewChipTitle: You named them yourself while building this world

tierTitle.unfleshed: Just a name so far. Give them a couple of lines, or interview them for a real voice.
tierTitle.extra: A background face: a couple of lines the narrator can use in passing.
tierTitle.named: Has a written profile. The narrator speaks them from it whenever they come up.

hub.promotionLine: Promotion in progress, seeded from the dossier.
hub.growNote: Each answer becomes one entry in the lore book. The card itself never changes.

agency.offLine: This world is cozy. It follows your lead.
agency.govNote: Written into the card, so it holds in any chat. Scenes can strain a hold, never break it.
agency.agendaPlaceholder: What this world pursues, regardless of the player...
agency.holdsPlaceholder: The lines that never bend, one per line...
agency.updateFailed: The agency could not be saved
agency.toggleFailed: The agency could not be switched
agency.offHint: Turn it on and the world quietly works toward a goal of its own and refuses to bend on its hard lines.

tuning.title: Weaver settings
tuning.subtitle: every field overrides a default; blank means use the default
tuning.intro: How much the studio does in one go. Leave a field blank to keep the default shown inside it.

fields.named_question_target: Questions before a profile is suggested
fields.dynamic_question_cap: Extra questions an interview may ask
fields.harvest_cap: Most people picked up from one build

hints.propose_count: How many people one Propose pass adds to a world's roster.
hints.named_question_target: Answers before a weave suggests saving the profile.
hints.dynamic_question_cap: Deepening questions after the essentials are covered.
hints.harvest_cap: People the roster picks up from your own words at finalize.
hints.generation_temperature: Everything the studio composes for you. Higher is looser.
hints.review_temperature: The quality checks and read-backs. Lower is steadier.

tuning.tempsIntro: How the model runs. Writing covers everything it composes for you; judging covers the quality checks.
